behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is required: rename detection depends on real `git` diff/status semantics and flags (e.g., rename heuristics), plus subprocess output parsing. A mocked git client can hide failures where the CLI invokes git incorrectly and receives add/delete instead of rename. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about actual renamed files in a repository, not just internal mapping logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert stdout report increments `renamed` only (no extra add/delete for that file).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both point to System: binary handling failures often appear in real git subprocess output/encoding paths, not in idealized fixtures. A mock diff string can miss real byte/marker behavior and subprocess decoding edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI process, assert exit code is success and report includes binary file in the correct count without traceback/crash.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git behavior on unborn HEAD/no-commit repositories (specific exit codes/stderr from subprocess calls). Integration with mocked git responses is plausible but can miss real command/exit handling. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI command, assert successful execution and empty structured report on stdout (no error output contract breach).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history; it depends on real git history traversal and subprocess data, not only local function logic. Mocked history can hide mismatches between git command behavior and parser/orchestrator assumptions.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real git repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run the CLI, and assert reported category counts exactly match expected history-derived counts.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Requires real index vs working-tree states and real git plumbing/flags; subprocess + repository state interaction is the failure boundary. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly contrasts staged and unstaged repository states that are git/OS-backed, not purely in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios (same content delta), run CLI for each scenario, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
